acceptance_targets: [ch15-f03] PASS if revised scene blocks corresponding to the cited lines contain no run of more than two consecutive paragraphs beginning with explicit clock stamps and at least four former time-stamp openers are replaced with non-clock sentence starts while chronology remains clear. FAIL if the chapter still reads as a repeated "At/By time" log.

[ch15-f04] PASS if in revised versions of the cited lines no more than two sentences use the exact "smelled like" construction and at least three replacements use non-olfactory sensory emphasis. FAIL if the same template continues to dominate those beats.

[ch15-f06] PASS if the revised span corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:441-465 includes at least three interiority beats from Ina and no uninterrupted stretch longer than nine paragraphs without interior experience. FAIL if the passage remains externally reported for 10+ consecutive paragraphs.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised scene blocks corresponding to the cited lines contain no run of more than two consecutive paragraphs beginning with explicit clock stamps and at least four former time-stamp openers are replaced with non-clock sentence starts while chronology remains clear. FAIL if the chapter still reads as a repeated "At/By time" log.
findings.problem: Recurring timestamp opener formula ("At/By HH:MM") appears far beyond twice, producing incident-log cadence and flattening sentence variety.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep only pivotal time anchors, but rewrite several openings at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:19, :159, :349, :419, :457, and :571 into action- or sensation-led entries that imply elapsed time without repeating identical syntax.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in revised versions of the cited lines no more than two sentences use the exact "smelled like" construction and at least three replacements use non-olfactory sensory emphasis. FAIL if the same template continues to dominate those beats.
findings.problem: A single sensory construction repeats six times ("X smelled like ..."), which becomes a prose tic and dulls freshness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the sensory sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:7, :147, :179, :213, :273, and :523 with mixed syntax and modality (touch/sound/spatial pressure) so smell is selective rather than default.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:441-465 includes at least three interiority beats from Ina and no uninterrupted stretch longer than nine paragraphs without interior experience. FAIL if the passage remains externally reported for 10+ consecutive paragraphs.
findings.problem: The chapter contains a 10+ paragraph run in midday updates that gives almost no focalizer interiority, shifting the read toward incident transcript rather than close-third embodiment.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the run starting at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:441 and continuing through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:465, insert brief thought/sensation/self-assessment beats tied to Ina's immediate tasks without slowing scene velocity.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
